original_image_tags: The document is a single-page typewritten memorandum on aged paper with some staining and creases, particularly on the right side. There are handwritten notations in the top right corner: "B/A" and a circled "8". A handwritten checkmark or initial appears near the bottom right corner next to the signature block.

elements.description: Typed memorandum dated 7 August 1947, Code 110A NRL. Subject: 'Flying Saucers'. From William P. Mellen, Major, Air Corps, to Asst Chief of Air Staff-2, Collection Branch, AAF, Washington 25, D.C. Mentions an enclosed excerpt from a letter to Dr. E. Tousey regarding unidentified objects seen by Mr. John F. Cole. Includes a signature for William P. Mellen and a handwritten annotation '3' in the top left corner.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the page is a single typed memorandum with no photographs, sketches, or diagrams, and both agree there are handwritten notations, though the specific content of those notations differs (descriptions of non-image content).
